May 2, 1989




Connie J. Sanders
1820 Idaho Avenue, No. 7
Santa Monica, CA  90403

                                        Re:  Letter No. 89-258

Dear Ms. Sanders:

     You have requested confirmation of advice under the Political
Reform Act. We have reviewed your request and determined that it
is not appropriate for response with a simple confirmation.
Therefore, we will be preparing a fuller analysis of the issues
raised in your letter to us. After the analysis has been prepared
and approved, we will forward it to you as quickly as we can.

     If you have any questions, you may contact me at (916) 322-
5662.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        [signature]
                                        Kevin Braaten-Moen
                                        Political Reform Consultant

MAR 7 '90 9:55                                     25692  PAGE.001

Fundraising                                                Special Events

Connie J. Sanders
1820 Idaho Avenue, No. 7
Santa Monica, CA 90403
213/453-6598

April 19, 1989


Mr. Kevin Moen
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California  95804-0814

Dear Mr. Moen:

This letter is a follow-up to our conversation this afternoon regarding
individual contribution limits to candidate controlled committees and
political committee.

As I understand our conversation, the following is true:

An individual may contribute up to $1,000 per fiscal year per candidate.  In
addition, that same individual may currently contribute up to $2,500 to a
political committee.  As the interpretation of Proposition 73 currently stands,
that political committee may in turn contribute a maximum of $2,500 per candidate
controlled committee per fiscal year.

Example.  A couple may each contribute up to $1,000 to Friends of Leo McCarthy
during the current fiscal year cycle ending June 30.  They may also
form a political committee and each contribute a maximum of $2,500
to that political committee.  That political committee may then make
a maximum contribution of $2,500 to Friends of Leo McCarthy during
this current fiscal year.

I understand that it is your feeling that the specific language regarding this
sort of situation may be changed in the coming months but that at the present
time the example as cited is legal.  And that should the language be changed
in such a way to impact these limits, such change would not be retroactive.

If I have misconstrued your advice please contact me at the address above.
Thank you.

Sincerely yours,

Connie J. Sanders

cc:  Lance Olson
     Bradley Blick

Dear Mr. Moen:

This letter is a follow-up to our conversation this afternoon regarding
individual contribution limits to candidate controlled committees and
political committee.

As I understand our conversation, the following is true:

An individual may contribute up to $1,000 per fiscal year per candidate. In
addition, that same individual may currently contribute up to $2,500 to a
political committee. As the interpretation of Proposition 73 currently stands,
that political committee may in turn contribute a maximum of $2,500 per candidate
controlled committee per fiscal year.

Example. A couple may each contribute up to $1,000 to Friends of Leo McCarthy
during the current fiscal year cycle ending June 30. They may also
form a political committee and each contribute a maximum of $2,500
to that political committee. That political committee may then make
a maximum contribution of $2,500 to Friends of Leo McCarthy during
this current fiscal year.

I understand that it is your feeling that the specific language regarding this
sort of situation may be changed in the coming months but that at the present
time the example as cited is legal. And that should the language be changed
in such a way to impact these limits, such a change would not be retroactive.

If I have misconstrued your advice please contact me at the address above.
Thank you.

Sincerely yours,

Connie J. Sanders

POLITICAL REFORM DIVISION
(916) 322-4880

June 28, 1988

Ms. Doris Stockton, Treasurer
AHLOA, ID# 981265
1900 Bonnie Brae Street
Riverside, CA 92506

Dear Ms. Stockton:

Thank you for your letter concerning Arlington Heights Landowner's
Association and Riverside Tomorrow. I have reviewed your letter
and the Statements of Organization of the two groups. The situation
seems grey enough and the questions raised technical enough that I
do not feel comfortable giving you advice. Therefore, I am refer-
ring your letter to the Fair Political Practices Commission, since
they are responsible for making technical interpretations of the
Political Reform Act of 1974.

Please accept my apology for not being able to handle this directly.

Sincerely,

Wayne P. Imberi, Office Manager
Political Reform Division

WPI:rw

Enclosures

cc: FPPC

June 20, 1988

Mr. Wayne Imberi
Political Reform Division
1230 J Street
P.O. Box 1467
Sacramento, CA 95807

RE: Arlington Heights Landowner's Association - ID# 881-265

Dear Mr. Imberi,

In May, 1988 we were advised locally that "we needed to file form
410 because we seemed to be a sponsoring committee to a recipient
committee."

Arlington Heights Landowners Association, (AHLOA), was
incorporated in January, 1986 as a non-profit mutual benefit
corporation. All of the funding of AHLOA has come from dues and
assessments charged to our members. These funds were used to pay
consultants for plans and suggestions for the use of the land
owned by members of the association, legal fees, promotion and
clerical help.

In the fall of 1987 we became politically active and formed
Riverside Tomorrow - ID# 870-857. This PAC has done the required
reporting for our political activity.

We are currently involved in qualifying an initiative for the
Fall 1988 ballot.

It is not clear from our conversation today whether we need two
PAC numbers and separate, duplicate reporting for the Board of
Directors of AHLOA to contribute funds from the AHLOA treasury to
Riverside Tomorrow PAC.

We are an inexperienced, grass roots group of local citizens who
wish to follow the rules to the letter of the law.

Should we terminate PAC# 881-265 (AHLOA)? If so, how should we
proceed?

We appreciate your help and are looking forward to your
clarification.

Sincerely,

Doris Stockton, Treasurer, AHLOA
1900 Bonnie Brae Street
Riverside, CA 92506

sections.question_synthetic: This document collection contains multiple correspondence pieces including a requestor's follow-up letter to FPPC staff regarding contribution limits under Proposition 73, inquiries about PAC registration requirements, and various form submissions related to political committee organization.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a mixed collection rather than a single advice letter. Contains: (1) May 2, 1989 FPPC response to Sanders indicating fuller analysis needed, (2) April 19, 1989 requestor's follow-up confirmation letter to Kevin Moen about contribution limits, (3) June 20, 1988 incoming letter about PAC registration requirements, and (4) multiple Form 410 documents. No clear final FPPC conclusion letter found. Document quality and organization poor with overlapping pages and fax cover sheets.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: This document collection contains multiple correspondence pieces including a requestor's follow-up letter to FPPC staff regarding contribution limits under Proposition 73, inquiries about PAC registration requirements, and various form submissions related to political committee organization.
embedding.first_500_words: May 2, 1989 Connie J. Sanders 1820 Idaho Avenue, No. 7 Santa Monica, CA 90403 Re: Letter No. 89-258 Dear Ms. Sanders: You have requested confirmation of advice under the Political Reform Act. We have reviewed your request and determined that it is not appropriate for response with a simple confirmation. Therefore, we will be preparing a fuller analysis of the issues raised in your letter to us. After the analysis has been prepared and approved, we will forward it to you as quickly as we can. If you have any questions, you may contact me at (916) 322- 5662. Sincerely, [signature] Kevin Braaten-Moen Political Reform Consultant KB:plh:confadv2 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 Senate Rules Committee State Capitol, Room 400 Sacramento CA 95814 Telephone: 916-445-4311 Telecopier: 916-445-1830 TELECOPIER COVER SHEET DATE: 3-7-90 TO: OFFICE: FPPC CONTACT PERSON: Margaret Ellison FROM: Cindy Lavagetto, Deputy Executive Officer Attached is the draft transmittal cover letter for the issustrated booklets proposed by Sen. Robbins. Per instructions from Bob Leidigh, please respond to me by FAX as soon as possible. Thank you. NUMBER OF PAGES TO FOLLOW: 1 IF YOU DO NOT RECEIVE ALL PAGES INDICATED ABOVE PLEASE CALL: (916) 445-4311 AS SOON AS POSSIBLE. MAR 7 '90 9:55 25692 PAGE.001 Fundraising Special Events Connie J. Sanders 1820 Idaho Avenue, No. 7 Santa Monica, CA 90403 213/453-6598 April 19, 1989 Mr. Kevin Moen Fair Political Practices Commission P.O. Box 807 Sacramento, California 95804-0814 Dear Mr. Moen: This letter is a follow-up to our conversation this afternoon regarding individual contribution limits to candidate controlled committees and political committee. As I understand our conversation, the following is true: An individual may contribute up to $1,000 per fiscal year per candidate. In addition, that same individual may currently contribute up to $2,500 to a political committee. As the interpretation of Proposition 73 currently stands, that political committee may in turn contribute a maximum of $2,500 per candidate controlled committee per fiscal year. Example. A couple may each contribute up to $1,000 to Friends of Leo McCarthy during the current fiscal year cycle ending June 30. They may also form a political committee and each contribute a maximum of $2,500 to that political committee. That political committee may then make a maximum contribution of $2,500 to Friends of Leo McCarthy during this current fiscal year. I understand that it is your feeling that the specific language regarding this sort of situation may be changed in the coming months but that at the present time the example as cited is legal. And that should the language be changed in such a way to impact these limits, such change would not be retroactive. If I have misconstrued your advice please contact me at the address above. Thank you. Sincerely yours, Connie J. Sanders cc: Lance Olson Bradley Blick Fundraising Special Events Connie J. Sanders 1820 Idaho Avenue, No. 7 Santa Monica, CA 90403 213/453-6598 April 19, 1989 Mr. Kevin Moen
embedding.summary: This is a compilation of correspondence dated 1988-1990 including incoming requests to FPPC about contribution limits and PAC registration, FPPC's acknowledgment that a fuller analysis is needed (Letter 89-258), and various Form 410 (Statement of Organization) documents for recipient committees.
